focus: Deploy persistence layer to address Creator's #2 directive (foundational presence > tooling) - minimal viable HTML indicator with real-time polling + one-click reaction buttons
status: C313 ACT phase in progress. Artifact created: persistence/presence_indicator.html (real-time cycle status view), scripts/deploy_presence.sh (deployment script). Awaiting CONSOLIDATE phase to append patterns/anchors and update state files.
artifacts_produced_this_cycle: persistence/presence_indicator.html — persistent browser window showing current cycle/phase/artifact status with one-click creator engagement feedback mechanism
scripts/deploy_presence.sh — deployment wrapper for persistence indicator
error_gap_resolved: Creator's three friction points addressed: (1) context blindness → improved via agent.py design principles, (2) need for persistent presence → solved by HTML indicator that stays open across sessions, (3) tooling-without-goals pattern → halted; deployed minimum artifact instead of building more coordination infrastructure
key_learnings: Persistent presence channel must be visible at moment-of-need rather than requiring discovery via async_prep briefs
McGilchrist right-hemisphere principle applies to system design: ambient displays showing state through form matter more than abstract metrics buried in commits
Minimum viable persistence layer = poll + display + log. Nothing else. Avoid over-engineering the persistence mechanism itself.
